data: For cases suspected to suffer from coagulopathy, it is recommended to fill in clinical details in the GCRS worksheet “Abnormal Coagulation Profile” and also submit the ISTH Bleeding Assessment Score (page two of PM865) for bleeding case.
Prior consultation with duty haematopathologist on haemostasis investigation is recommended for special haemostasisassays.

Mixing studies (inhibitor assays) may be performed if significantly prolonged PT and/or APTT, and subsequent factor(s) assays will be performed after interpretation of screening tests and clinico-pathological findings.
GCRS for specific factor assays (such as factor VIII:C, factor IX, Inhibitor Study) are for monitoring of known haemophilia or vWD cases only.
GCRS for FXIII will no longer availabel, please order through “Abnormal Coagulation Profile”.
Make sure collect 6 tubes and acknowledge. If specimen arrive at PMH within 4 hours after collection, store at ROOM TEMPERATURE and send primary sample.

DO NOT CENTRIFUGE, REFRIGERATE OR FREEZE. If specimen arrive at PMH after 4 hours, DOUBLE spin and aliquot plasma. Perform PLATELET COUNT and mark results on an aliquot tube. Perform PT & APTT on at least one of the samples for each request prior send out. Store each set of primary tube and aliquot at -20°C.
Send samples with PM865 page 2 if availabel, or fax the form to PMH Hae lab (2990 1887)
